params.text: Around the 2020-11-15 failed-logon activity, at least one Security 4624 successful logon event was present but it was a service logon, not a remote logon: TargetUserName=SYSTEM and LogonType=5 with IpAddress=-.

params.justification: Multiple searches for 4624 on 2020-11-15 specifically for LogonType=3 and LogonType=10 returned zero hits (audit_id sift-root-20260617-041 and sift-root-20260617-043). The only examined 4624 record around the failed-logon timeframe shows LogonType=5 and IpAddress='-' (record_id 2404665).
params.text: The indexed Security 4624 activity on 2020-11-15 does not corroborate a successful remote logon during the observed 4625 burst; the only retrieved 4624 record is LogonType=5 (service) with IpAddress='-'. Additional searching for LogonType=3 (network) and LogonType=10 (RDP) on that date returned no hits in the evidence index, suggesting remote interactive access via RDP was not successfully established (or relevant event sources were not ingested/available).
params.what_would_change_this_confidence: If additional Security EVTX segments for 2020-11-15 (or DC Security logs, if authentication was domain-based) are ingested and contain 4624 events with LogonType=3/10, or if TerminalServices Operational logs for 2020-11-15 show successful RDP session creation (e.g., event IDs 21/22/25), then this conclusion would change.

cited_spans.span_text: LNK target=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst workdir=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST
EventID=31 Application provider=Outlook computer=SRL-FORGE Data=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst

params.text: LNK artifacts for user fredr reference a PST email export located in Google Drive (“My Drive”).

params.justification: The evidence explicitly shows the PST path on G:\My Drive and an Outlook Application log entry referencing the same PST, tying the file to user activity. However, LNK/Application logs alone do not show whether the PST was newly created, what its contents were, or whether it synchronized off-host.
params.text: This indicates Outlook accessed (and likely loaded/indexed) a mailbox export file (SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst) stored under a Google Drive “My Drive” path, which is consistent with staging email data into a cloud-synced location for transfer/off-host access. This supports Q2 (what data) and Q3 (where transferred), but does not by itself prove the PST was created or uploaded during the session.
params.what_would_change_this_confidence: Raising confidence would require corroboration such as MFT/USN records showing the PST file creation/modify timestamps and size changes, GoogleDriveFS/Drive sync logs, SRUM network usage for GoogleDriveFS.exe, or firewall/proxy logs showing upload traffic around the same time.

params.text: No SRUM (srum:network_usage) entries for GoogleDriveFS/OneDrive/Dropbox were found in the requested 2020-11-14 13:30–14:10Z window via index search for timestamps containing that window; however, there is non-SRUM telemetry indicating Google DriveFS activity and OneDrive execution near that time, and Dropbox update artifacts at 13:50Z. Supporting records are cited.

In the 2020-11-14 13:30–14:10Z timeframe, the evidence index contains no SRUM Network Usage ("SRUM network_usage") records for GoogleDriveFS/OneDrive/Dropbox that include bytes_sent/bytes_recvd; searches for timestamp tokens "2020-11-14T13"/"2020-11-14T14" in conjunction with "SRUM network_usage" returned zero hits.
